description: Session Reconnection & Partner Presence - Students can disconnect and reconnect without losing progress, partners are notified of disconnections, instructors see presence status and can move stranded students

userStories.title: Add presence data structure to room blob and heartbeat endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I need per-student presence tracking stored in the room blob so that both clients and the dashboard can determine who is online.
acceptanceCriteria: Add presence field to makeEmptyRoom in workshop-session.js: presence: { student1: { online: false, lastSeen: null }, student2: { online: false, lastSeen: null } }
Update workshop-heartbeat.js to write the student's lastSeen (ISO timestamp) and online: true into the room blob's presence object
Identify which student slot (student1/student2) the heartbeating student occupies by matching studentName against room.students
Add presence_timeout: 30000 to WORKSHOP_CONFIG in src/lib/config.js
Old rooms without presence field are handled gracefully (initialize presence if missing on heartbeat write)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The existing heartbeat writes to a separate heartbeat:{sessionId}:{roomId} blob. This story adds a write to the main room blob's presence field as well. Use the same read-modify-write pattern used elsewhere in the codebase.

userStories.title: Add presence data to workshop-pulse and workshop-room responses
userStories.description: As a developer, I need presence data returned in API responses so the client and dashboard can render online/offline status.
acceptanceCriteria: workshop-room.js GET response includes the room's presence object
If a room has no presence field (old rooms), normalize it to { student1: { online: false, lastSeen: null }, student2: { online: false, lastSeen: null } }
workshop-pulse.js response includes per-room presence data in each room summary
A student is considered offline if lastSeen is null or older than 30 seconds from current time
Pulse response includes a computed online boolean per student based on the 30s threshold
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The pulse endpoint returns lightweight summaries. Adding presence is just 2 booleans + 2 timestamps per room — minimal payload increase. Compute the online boolean server-side so clients don't need to duplicate the threshold logic.

userStories.title: Create reusable Toast notification component
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a toast component for showing brief auto-dismissing notifications across the app.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/lib/components/Toast.svelte that accepts message (string), duration (ms, default 5000), and type (info/success/warning) props
Toast appears in the bottom-right corner of the viewport with position: fixed
Toast auto-dismisses after the specified duration
Toast has a subtle slide-in animation on appear and fade-out on dismiss
Uses existing CSS variables (--ci-surface, --ci-text, --ci-border, --ci-accent) for theming
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This toast will be used for 'Welcome back', 'Partner is back online', and 'You've been moved' notifications in later stories. Keep it simple — no stacking or queue needed, just one toast at a time.

userStories.title: Show partner disconnection banner on interview page
userStories.description: As a student, I want to see a non-blocking banner when my partner disconnects so I know what's happening and can continue working.
acceptanceCriteria: When polling workshop-room detects partner's presence.online is false (or lastSeen > 30s), show a yellow/amber banner at the top of the interview area
Banner text: 'Your partner appears to be disconnected. You can continue working — they'll be able to rejoin.'
Banner does not block or freeze any UI — student can continue typing notes, navigating phases
Banner auto-dismisses when partner's presence flips back to online
Track partner's previous online state to detect transitions (offline→online and online→offline)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The interview page already polls workshop-room. Use the presence data from that response. The banner should be a simple conditional element pinned below the header, not a modal or overlay.

userStories.title: Show partner reconnection toast
userStories.description: As a student, I want to see a brief toast when my disconnected partner comes back online.
acceptanceCriteria: When partner transitions from offline to online (was offline, now online), show Toast with message: 'Your partner is back online!'
Toast uses the Toast component from US-003 with type 'success'
Toast auto-dismisses after 5 seconds
Toast does not show on initial page load (only on transition from offline→online)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-003 (Toast component) and US-004 (presence tracking on interview page). The offline→online transition detection from US-004 can trigger this toast.

userStories.title: Autosave on beforeunload and hydrate from server on reconnect
userStories.description: As a student, I want my in-progress work automatically saved when I disconnect and restored from the server when I reconnect.
acceptanceCriteria: On beforeunload event, use navigator.sendBeacon to POST current notes text to workshop-submit endpoint
The sendBeacon payload includes sessionId, roomId, studentName, round, and current notes text
On reconnect (page load with valid ws_* localStorage keys), the existing resume flow fetches room state from server
Server-side submission data takes priority over localStorage for notes content — client hydrates textarea from server data
If server has newer submission for current round, use it; otherwise fall back to localStorage
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: navigator.sendBeacon is fire-and-forget but more reliable than fetch during page teardown. The existing debounced save (1500ms) handles continuous typing; this covers the tab-close edge case. The resume flow already exists — this story enhances it to prefer server state.

userStories.title: Show welcome-back toast on reconnection
userStories.description: As a student who disconnected, I want to see a brief confirmation of where I am after reconnecting.
acceptanceCriteria: After successful reconnection via the existing resume flow, show Toast: 'Welcome back! You're in Round [X] as [Interviewer/Storyteller].'
Toast uses the Toast component from US-003 with type 'info'
Toast auto-dismisses after 5 seconds
Only shows when resuming a session (rejoined: true from workshop-join), not on fresh joins
Current round, phase (notes/followup/profile), and role are correctly displayed in the toast
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The resume flow already restores state. This story just adds the toast notification after successful resume. The role is determined by the existing determineRoles function.

userStories.title: Add presence indicators to instructor dashboard
userStories.description: As an instructor, I want to see green/red dots next to student names showing who is online or offline.
acceptanceCriteria: Each student name in the dashboard room cards has a small colored dot (8-10px circle): green for online, red for offline
Offline students show a gray timestamp below their name: 'offline since [time]' (e.g., 'offline since 2:34 PM')
Presence data comes from the workshop-pulse response (added in US-002), updating on the existing 2s polling cycle
Students with no presence data (empty rooms, old rooms) show no dot
Presence indicators update reactively without full page refresh
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The dashboard already polls workshop-pulse every 2s. This story consumes the presence data added in US-002 and renders it visually next to student names in the room cards.

userStories.title: Create workshop-move-student API endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a server endpoint that atomically moves a student from one room to another.
acceptanceCriteria: Create netlify/functions/workshop-move-student.js that accepts POST with { sessionId, studentName, fromRoomId, toRoomId }
Removes the student from the source room (clears their student slot and email, resets their presence)
Adds the student to the target room's first available slot (student1 or student2)
If target room is full (both slots occupied), return 409 with error message
Sets a movedTo field on the source room's student slot so stale client writes to the old room are rejected
Sets a movedFrom field on the target room's student slot so the client can detect the move
Preserves the student's existing submissions in the source room (does not delete them)
Resets the target room's currentRound to match where both students should restart
Returns { success: true, newRoom: { ...targetRoomData } }
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use read-modify-write on both room blobs. The movedTo/movedFrom fields are important for client-side detection. The target room's currentRound should be set to the moved student's currentRound so the round restarts fresh.

userStories.title: Add Move Student button and dialog to instructor dashboard
userStories.description: As an instructor, I want to move a stranded student to a different room when their partner has disconnected.
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Move Student' button appears on room cards where one student is offline for more than 2 minutes and the other is online
Button is styled as a secondary/warning action using existing .ws-btn .ws-btn--secondary classes
Clicking the button opens a dialog listing available target rooms (rooms with 0 or 1 students)
Each target room option shows room number and current student name (if any)
Selecting a target room shows a confirmation: 'Move [StudentName] to Room [X]?'
On confirmation, POST to workshop-move-student endpoint with the correct parameters
On success, dashboard refreshes to reflect the move
On error (409 room full), show an error message
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The 2-minute offline threshold should be computed from the presence lastSeen timestamp in the pulse data. Only show the button for the online student — moving the offline student is not supported.

userStories.title: Detect room change on interview page client
userStories.description: As a student who has been moved by the instructor, I want my interview page to detect the room change automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: The interview page poll cycle checks for a movedTo field in the workshop-room response for the student's current room
Alternatively, check if the student's name is no longer in the current room's students object
On detecting a move, fetch the new room data using the movedTo roomId
Update localStorage ws_roomId to the new room ID
Clear any stale state from the previous room (notes, phase, partner name)
Transition to the partner change screen (US-012) instead of directly to the interview
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The movedTo field is set by the workshop-move-student endpoint (US-009). Poll cycle already hits workshop-room — just need to check the response for move indicators.

userStories.title: Partner change transition screen for moved student
userStories.description: As a student who was moved to a new room, I want to see a transition screen explaining what happened before the round restarts.
acceptanceCriteria: When a room change is detected (US-011), show a transition screen instead of jumping directly to the interview
Screen displays: 'You've been paired with a new partner! You'll be starting Round [X] together.'
Screen shows the new partner's name (fetched from the new room's students object)
A 'Ready' button lets the student acknowledge the change
Clicking 'Ready' writes a ready flag to the room blob via a new API call or by reusing workshop-heartbeat with a ready parameter
If both students have clicked Ready, or 10 seconds have elapsed, transition to the interview screen
The round starts fresh: empty notes textarea, roles re-determined by alphabetical sort of the new pair
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: This is the moved student's view. The existing student in the target room sees a similar screen (US-013). The ready flag can be stored in the room blob's presence or a new readyForRound field.

userStories.title: Partner change transition screen for existing student
userStories.description: As a student whose room just received a new partner, I want to see a transition screen so I'm not confused by a sudden partner change.
acceptanceCriteria: When the interview page poll detects a new student in the room (partner name changed or movedFrom field present on new student), show a transition screen
Screen displays: 'A new partner has joined your room! You'll be restarting Round [X] together.'
Screen shows the new partner's name
A 'Ready' button lets the student acknowledge
Clicking 'Ready' writes a ready flag to the room blob
If both students have clicked Ready, or 10 seconds have elapsed, transition to the interview screen
The round restarts fresh: empty notes textarea, roles re-determined by alphabetical sort of the new pair
Any in-progress work from the interrupted round is preserved in submissions history (not deleted)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: This mirrors US-012 but from the existing student's perspective. The detection trigger is different: this student's room poll detects a new partner appearing, not a movedTo flag. Both students must click Ready (or wait 10s) before the round restarts.
